business.name: Black Creek Smokehouse
business.tagline: Pit-smoked since 1987. Third generation. Still family.

sampleQuotes: Been coming here since I was a kid. The pulled pork hasn't changed in 30 years.
Eddie Ray still tends the pit himself. You can taste the difference.

brand.colorDirection: Warm wood tones, cream, burnt orange

content.heroSubtext: Three generations. One smoker. The best BBQ in Cleveland County.

services.name: Whole Hog BBQ
services.description: 24-hour pit-smoked whole hog. Pulled or chopped. Served with our 1987 sauce recipe.

services.name: St. Louis Ribs
services.description: Dry-rubbed and smoked over hickory. Fall-off-the-bone tender.

services.name: Smoked Brisket
services.description: 12-hour smoked Angus brisket. Sliced to order. Limited quantities daily.

services.name: Smoked Wings
services.description: Jumbo wings smoked then flash-fried. Tossed in your choice of our house sauces.

services.name: Family Platter
services.description: Feeds 4-6. Choice of 2 meats, 4 sides, cornbread, and a gallon of sweet tea.

testimonials.quote: Best pulled pork I've had in North Carolina. And I've tried a lot of pulled pork.
testimonials.author: Mike T.

testimonials.quote: Black Creek is our family tradition. Every Sunday after church. The brisket and mac and cheese are unbeatable.
testimonials.author: Lisa R.

testimonials.quote: Eddie Ray came out and talked to us for 20 minutes. You can tell he genuinely loves what he does. The food proves it.
testimonials.author: Chris D.

content.story: Eddie Ray Coleman built his first smoker out of a 55-gallon drum in 1987. He was 22 years old, working at the textile mill by day and selling barbecue out of his driveway on weekends. Word spread. By 1992, the line stretched down Buffalo Creek Road every Saturday. His son Travis grew up tending the pit alongside him — learning which wood burns hottest, when to wrap, when to rest. Today, they run the Smokehouse together. Eddie Ray still lights the pit every morning at 3am. Travis runs the kitchen. His daughter Maddie handles the front of house. The recipes are the same ones Eddie Ray's mother wrote down on a notecard in 1965.
sellingPoints: Three generations of pitmasters — the Coleman family has been smoking meat since 1987
Whole hog BBQ cooked low and slow for 24 hours over hickory and oak
Every sauce, side, and dessert made from scratch daily
Outdoor picnic seating under the pecan trees

meta.currentWebPresence: None — this is a faux portfolio showcase
